itinerary.dayDescription: Arrival in Paris, iconic landmarks, and a taste of classic French dining.

morning.details: Begin your Parisian adventure with breathtaking views of the Eiffel Tower from the Trocadéro Gardens. Enjoy the iconic landmark and take plenty of photos.
morning.address: Place du Trocadéro et du 11 Novembre, 75016 Paris, France
morning.ticketPricing: Free for views from Trocadéro. Eiffel Tower ascent: €11.30-€29.40 (Focus on exterior views for mid-budget).
morning.timeToTravel: Approx. 30-45 minutes by Metro from central areas.
morning.bestTimeToVisit: Morning for fewer crowds and good light for photos.

afternoon.details: Immerse yourselves in art and history at the world's largest art museum. Focus on key masterpieces like the Mona Lisa and Venus de Milo, and explore historical sections.
afternoon.timeToTravel: Approx. 15-20 minutes by Metro from the Eiffel Tower area.
afternoon.bestTimeToVisit: Afternoon, allow 3-4 hours to explore the highlights.

evening.details: Enjoy classic French cuisine in the charming and historic Saint-Germain-des-Prés district. Afterward, take a romantic evening stroll along the Seine River, crossing Pont Neuf.
evening.timeToTravel: Metro to Mabillon or Saint-Germain-des-Prés station.

localTips: Purchase a 'carnet' of 10 Metro tickets for convenience and a slight discount.
Always say 'Bonjour' when entering a shop or restaurant, and 'Merci' when leaving.
Be aware of your surroundings, especially in crowded tourist areas, to avoid pickpockets.

itinerary.dayDescription: Discover the historical heart of Paris and explore the charming Le Marais district.

morning.placeName: Île de la Cité: Notre Dame Cathedral (Exterior) & Sainte-Chapelle
morning.details: Visit the historical island. Observe the ongoing restoration of Notre Dame from its exterior, then marvel at the exquisite stained glass windows of Sainte-Chapelle.
morning.address: 6 Parvis Notre-Dame - Pl. Jean-Paul II, 75004 Paris, France (Notre Dame) | 8 Boulevard du Palais, 75001 Paris, France (Sainte-Chapelle)
morning.ticketPricing: Notre Dame exterior: Free. Sainte-Chapelle: €11.50 per person.
morning.bestTimeToVisit: Morning for Sainte-Chapelle to avoid the longest queues.

afternoon.placeName: Le Marais District (Place des Vosges, Jewish Quarter)
afternoon.details: Wander through Le Marais, one of Paris's oldest and most beautiful neighborhoods. Explore Place des Vosges, browse boutiques, and discover the historic Jewish Quarter with its famous falafel.
afternoon.address: Place des Vosges, 75004 Paris, France (for reference)
afternoon.ticketPricing: Free to explore. Food costs extra (e.g., falafel around €8-€10).
afternoon.timeToTravel: Walk from Île de la Cité (approx. 15 minutes) or Metro to Saint-Paul.
afternoon.bestTimeToVisit: Afternoon for a leisurely exploration and food stops.

evening.details: Enjoy dinner at one of Le Marais' many charming bistros or try the famous falafel on Rue des Rosiers for a casual and delicious experience.
evening.address: Rue des Rosiers, 75004 Paris, France (for falafel reference)

localTips: Try a traditional French crêpe from a street vendor for a sweet or savory snack.
Don't be afraid to try the local markets for fresh produce and gourmet treats.
Many smaller shops in Le Marais might close on Sundays, but falafel places are usually open.

itinerary.dayDescription: Explore artistic Montmartre and get some last-minute Parisian views before departure.

morning.details: Ascend to the artistic district of Montmartre. Visit the stunning Sacré-Cœur Basilica for panoramic city views and soak in the bohemian atmosphere of Place du Tertre, where artists display their work.
morning.address: 35 Rue du Chevalier de la Barre, 75018 Paris, France (Sacré-Cœur)
morning.ticketPricing: Basilica: Free. Dome access: €7 per person. Funicular up Montmartre: Metro ticket price.
morning.timeToTravel: Metro to Anvers or Abbesses, then funicular or walk up.
morning.bestTimeToVisit: Morning for best light and to avoid peak crowds.

afternoon.details: Admire the magnificent architecture of the Opéra Garnier (exterior or interior if time allows). Then, head to Galeries Lafayette department store for a unique shopping experience and panoramic views of Paris from its rooftop terrace.
afternoon.address: Place de l'Opéra, 75009 Paris, France (Palais Garnier) | 40 Boulevard Haussmann, 75009 Paris, France (Galeries Lafayette)
afternoon.ticketPricing: Opera exterior: Free. Interior tour: €14 per person. Galeries Lafayette: Free.
afternoon.bestTimeToVisit: Early afternoon before heading to the train station.

evening.details: Head to Gare du Nord for your Eurostar journey back to London. Allow ample time for check-in and security.
evening.bestTimeToVisit: Allow at least 90 minutes before scheduled train departure.

localTips: If you're flying out, ensure you know which airport you're departing from (CDG or ORY) and plan your travel accordingly.
Consider grabbing a pain au chocolat and coffee from a local boulangerie for a quick and authentic Parisian breakfast.
Take a final moment to enjoy the city's ambiance from a café terrace.

hotels.description: Charming hotel directly facing the Panthéon, offering a fantastic location in the heart of the Latin Quarter. Ideal for history lovers, with classic Parisian decor and excellent metro access.
A stylish and artistic boutique hotel near the Louvre Museum and Palais Royal. Its central location makes it perfect for exploring major sights and enjoying Parisian culture.
Located in the vibrant Le Marais district, this hotel offers comfortable stays amidst historic streets, charming boutiques, and excellent food options. Close to Hôtel de Ville and the Seine.
